primary_reason: The paper develops and validates a rapid case-data processing approach for estimating growth rates, reproduction numbers, and medium-term epidemic trends.
The paper proposes an R0 estimation method that uses empirical incubation-period distributions rather than Markovian compartment assumptions.
The study develops and evaluates a reproduction-number model that adjusts surveillance incidence using diagnostic testing volume.
The study compares time-varying reproduction-number estimates across cases, hospitalizations, deaths, and healthcare sectors to assess surveillance robustness during COVID-19 waves.
The study estimates incubation period, generation time, and reproduction number from early confirmed monkeypox cases.
The title indicates an early monkeypox reproduction-number analysis, but the supplied record contains no abstract or methodological detail for a responsible role assignment.
The study develops and compares a multi-self-regression deep neural network for hospital infectious-disease early warning.
The study applies real-time reproduction-number estimation to evaluate COVID-19 movement restrictions and non-pharmaceutical interventions in Fiji.
The paper develops a time-series approach for recovering potential surfaces and diffusion functions to interpret epidemic early-warning signals.
The study improves inverse-problem reproduction-number estimation with robust data handling and Monte Carlo credibility intervals.
The study compares forecasting models and operational data inputs to build a practical dengue outbreak early-warning system.
The study constructs and evaluates a biomarker-enhanced clinical scoring model for early sepsis detection in older emergency-department patients.
The study uses changes in effective reproduction number and mobility to evaluate COVID-19 control measures in Taiwan.
The study evaluates T-cell subtype thresholds as early markers and prognostic indicators of active cytomegalovirus infection in sepsis.
The study applies a spatial vector-borne compartmental model to estimate yellow-fever reproduction numbers in a partially vaccinated population.
The article describes the workforce-development and technical-assistance activities of food-safety centers without evaluating a transferable surveillance or outbreak-detection method.
The paper provides a general method for deriving generation intervals and reproduction numbers from heterogeneous compartmental models using incidence data.
The study fits a sexual-network transmission model to explain sustained monkeypox transmission and quantify outbreak risk.
The study uses wave-stratified multilevel time-series models to estimate how policy stringency and mobility affected COVID-19 incidence and transmission.
The study develops visualization and machine-learning warning tools for national food security rather than an infectious-disease outcome with a concrete transfer route.
The systematic review and meta-analysis evaluates heterogeneity and study-method effects in early COVID-19 R0 estimates.
The case study teaches a structured surveillance-system evaluation approach for identifying causes of delayed malaria outbreak detection.
The study applies willingness-to-pay scenarios to value health gains from an international infectious-disease early-warning system.
The study evaluates an automated Twitter-based threat-detection tool against manual monitoring for sensitivity, predictive value, geolocation, and timeliness.
The study applies a fitted SIR model to estimate reproduction number and evacuation scenarios for a confined COVID-19 outbreak.
The paper presents EpiLPS, a fast Bayesian P-spline framework for time-varying reproduction-number estimation with uncertainty intervals.
The systematic analysis fits negative-binomial branching models to genomic tuberculosis cluster sizes to quantify transmission and superspreading.
The study evaluates biomarker and pediatric early-warning-score combinations for risk stratification of febrile children with possible bacterial infection.
The paper develops and validates a scalable convolutional-neural-network method for detecting active HIV outbreaks from pairwise genetic-distance matrices.
The study estimates farm-level African swine fever reproduction numbers to assess whether spot removal can control outbreaks in different pig groups.
The paper derives and numerically evaluates a canonical R0 formula for compartmental epidemic processes on sparse contact networks.
The study operationalizes real-time EpiNow2 reproduction-number estimation to monitor a SARS-CoV-2 Delta outbreak and policy changes in New Zealand.
The paper develops a framework to quantify how genomic surveillance depth affects outbreak-cluster detection resolution and delay.
The study models activity-specific infection risk from GPS-derived population density to estimate COVID-19 reproduction numbers and target restrictions.
The study applies change-point analysis and ARIMA and neural-network forecasting to lumpy skin disease outbreak reports across continents.
The study identifies virulence-associated proteins in one Acinetobacter baumannii strain but does not present a transferable epidemiologic or surveillance design.
The study develops and externally validates multisource internet-data ensemble models for early warning of COVID-19 cryptic transmission.
The study evaluates event-based surveillance systems against virological influenza data across countries for detection accuracy and timeliness.
The study uses an open surveillance dataset to derive early country-level monkeypox R0 estimates in Europe.

The study develops a machine-learning warning system for myocardial infarction rather than an infectious-disease outcome with a concrete transfer route.

The review substantively summarizes principles, applications, advantages, and limitations of influenza surveillance and early-warning models.
This corrigendum corrects a prior article on estimating effective reproduction number using generation time versus serial interval.
